analysis.summary: The partial government shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security has become the longest in U.S. history, continuing as Congress departs on a two-week recess for Passover and Easter. House Republicans rejected a bipartisan Senate deal to reopen DHS, leaving TSA officers and other DHS employees without pay, though TSA workers began receiving retroactive paychecks. President Trump is urging Congress to cut its recess short to resolve the funding impasse.
